risk_title: Weak Project Steering Committee Engagement and Governance Guidance
risk_summary: The Project Steering Committee (PSC), chaired by the Minister of Labor and Skills and comprising key stakeholders, has only held one meeting to date. Critical project decisions on Skills for Jobs focus areas and priority subsectors for Sector Skills Bodies lack PSC guidance at a critical implementation stage, indicating potential governance fragility in project oversight.
evidence_quote: To date, only one meeting of the PSC has been held. The mission requests that the PSC meets regularly to guide implementation of the project. Several aspects of the project including focus areas for windows under the Skills for Jobs and priority subsectors for the Sector Skills Bodies (SSBs) need guidance from the PSC at this critical stage of project implementation.

risk_title: Staffing Gaps in Procurement and Contract Management
risk_summary: The project has been unable to successfully recruit a Procurement/Contract Specialist position after the initial recruitment process failed. This staffing gap affects implementation of major procurement activities worth over 18% of total project financing and may delay critical project activities.
evidence_quote: The current process was not able to identify a candidate successfully. which is currently at the negotiations stage. The mission also guided the team to begin planning to launch the next window by March, 2025 with guidance from the PSC... Finalize the recruitment of the Procurement/Contract Specialist by February 24, 2025.

risk_title: Inadequate Grievance Redress Mechanism and Community Representation
risk_summary: Grievance redress mechanisms for both beneficiary communities and project workers remain incomplete. Grievance committees lack representation from beneficiary communities including women, and focal persons have not received confidentiality training, creating risks for unaddressed complaints and potential community tensions.
evidence_quote: Going forward, the project needs to (i) finalize the grievance redress mechanism for both the beneficiary community as well as for the project workers; (ii) ensure that grievance committees (GRC) has the representation from the beneficiary community including women; (iii) train the focal person of SEA/SH GRC on confidentiality.

risk_title: Incomplete Security Management and SEA/SH Safeguarding Arrangements
risk_summary: Security management focal points have not been appointed at the federal level or within Project Implementation Teams (PITs) to address Sexual Exploitation, Abuse, and Harassment (SEA/SH) issues. Code of Conduct signing by project workers remains incomplete, creating vulnerabilities in worker and beneficiary protection.
evidence_quote: Going forward, the project needs to... (vi) appoint either a focal point or a consultant for security management at the federal level to train and guide PITs; and (vii) nominate a focal point in each of the PITs for addressal of SEA/SH issues, if any. The mission also advised the project to ensure that all workers sign the Code of Conduct on dos and don'ts.
